channel.title: The NHS Alliance - News
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for The NHS Alliance news

item.title: Focus on prevention to tackle falling healthy life expectancy
item.description: Dr Layla McCay responds to Health Foundation analysis showing that healthy life expectancy has fallen and the gap between affluent and deprived communities has grown.

item.title: "Helpful and timely" PAC report on the New Hospital Programme
item.description: Dr Layla McCay responds to the Public Accounts Committee's report on the New Hospital Programme.

item.title: Mental health performance target needed to address "appalling treatment gap"
item.description: Rebecca Gray calls for a new NHS mental health performance target for children and young people to help address an "appalling treatment gap" for people with mental ill health who can’t access care.

item.title: Genuine performance progress will make a difference for thousands of NHS patients
item.description: Rory Deighton responds to the latest NHS performance figures.

item.title: Health and Care Women Leaders Network responds to the government's renewed Women's Health Strategy
item.description: Salma Yasmeen, chair of the Health and Care Women Leaders Network, responds to the government's renewed Women's Health Strategy.

item.title: Health care is a team game with good admin at its heart
item.description: Dr Layla McCay responds to findings on NHS administration and communications.

item.title: Extra funding for Community Diagnostic Centres is welcome
item.description: Rory Deighton responds to the announcement of extra investment in new Community Diagnostic Centres.

item.title: Welcome support to end corridor care
item.description: Rory Deighton responds to steps announced by the government to help tackle corridor care.

item.title: NHS funding model switch would bring upheaval and disruption
item.description: Dr Layla McCay responds to IPPR report on NHS funding models.

item.title: Strike is "bitterly disappointing"
item.description: Rory Deighton comments on the latest strike by resident doctors.

item.title: NHS Confederation and NHS Providers respond to first 27 new neighbourhood health centres
item.description: Ruth Rankine responds to the announcement of the first 27 new neighbourhood health centres.

item.title: NHS Providers and NHS Confederation respond to British Social Attitudes survey
item.description: Rory Deighton, responds to the latest British Social Attitudes (BSA) survey.

item.title: NHS Providers and NHS Confederation respond to Intensive Recovery programme
item.description: Matthew Taylor responds to the announcement of an Intensive Recovery programme to support struggling NHS trusts.

item.title: Cost impact of resident doctor strikes terrible way to start new financial year
item.description: Rory Deighton responds to news that resident doctors plan to strike in April.

item.title: NHS Providers and NHS Confederation statement on Hatzola ambulance attacks
item.description: Matthew Taylor responds to the statement on the Hatzola ambulance attacks.

item.title: The government should act swiftly on Covid inquiry recommendations
item.description: Dr Layla McCay responds to the third report of the Covid-19 public inquiry.

item.title: Findings on the health of young people not in work 'concerning'
item.description: Rebecca Gray, mental health director speaking on behalf of the NHS Confederation and NHS Providers, responds to Health Foundation findings on the health of young people not in work.

item.title: Staff have a right to feel supported, valued, and respected at work
item.description: Rory Deighton responds to the 2025 NHS Staff Survey.

item.title: NHS leaders and their teams have pulled out all the stops to drive down waiting lists
item.description: Rory Deighton responds to the latest monthly performance statistics published by NHS England.

item.title: Underfunding mental health is not just bad for patients but for wider communities and economy, health leaders say
item.description: Rebecca Gray, mental health director speaking on behalf of the NHS Confederation and NHS Providers responded to the government’s announcement on mental health spending.
